summary: Ukrainian forces are using fiber-optic FPV drones with up to 50 km control range, making them resistant to jamming and capable of deep strikes on Russian rear areas. Russian forces are countering with log-periodic directional antennas on "Molniyas," operating on 3.3 GHz with vertical/horizontal polarization to resist jamming and evade interception. Ukrainian drones have begun targeting Russian supply lines and rear infrastructure, causing logistical collapse and forcing Russian forces into a defensive posture. Russian systems like "Elka" and "Yolka" are being used to intercept Ukrainian fixed-wing "Hornet" drones. On March 14, 2026, Rubicon Center operators conducted FPV anti-drone operations, eliminating 136 Ukrainian targets across multiple directions. A Russian FPV drone struck an ambulance in Chervona Khvylia, killing two medical workers and injuring another. Ukrainian FPV pilots are employing tactics such as throwing riders off horses before striking, and in one instance, a Russian soldier charging a drone with a bayonet was killed.
